channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Erin Brockovich takes aim at data center secrecy
item.description: Environmental activist Erin Brockovich has a new mission.

item.title: How to configure ssh-agent
item.description: How to configure ssh-agent, agent forwarding, & agent protocol The ssh-agent is a helper program that keeps track of users' identity keys and their passphrases. The agent can then use the keys to...

item.title: How I used Inkscape to make Assets for GitHub and DEV.to
item.description: Developers often ignore their profile picture by keeping default geometric placeholders or blank banners because design tools can be intimidating. Up until recently, my GitHub profile was simply a...

item.title: I Published an AI Memory Result. Then Real Retrieval Broke Everything.
item.description: This is a submission for the GitHub Finish-Up-A-Thon Challenge. What I Built I published an AI memory result. Then I added real retrieval and everything broke. This is what I built after I went...

item.title: I sketched 29 agentic AI design patterns in a Da Vinci–style notebook (open source)
item.description: Spent the weekend turning every pattern in my agentic_design_patterns repo into a hand-drawn style diagram, using a sepia/parchment “bozza-scientifica” aesthetic — closer to a Leonardo da Vinci...

item.title: Why I Stopped Letting AI Write My Code (May 2026)
item.description: This article shares why a web engineer holding a Claude Code Max 20x plan has mostly stopped using AI for coding at work. It's not about abandoning AI — it's about narrowing down where I let AI...

item.title: 2026 Has 53 Weeks. Here's the Bug That's About to Surface.
item.description: TL;DR 2026 is a 53 week year (ISO 8601). If anything in your stack assumes a year has 52 weeks a % 52, a fixed length array, a chart axis, a weekly aggregation it has a latent bug that this year...

item.title: Building Your First LangGraph Pipeline: A Decision-Maker's Guide
item.description: LangGraph is becoming the default framework for teams building agentic AI workflows. That is both a good thing and a problem. The good part: it has real production pedigree, is actively...

item.title: The state of LLMs — June 2026
item.description: 1|--- 2|title: "The state of LLMs — June 2026" 3|description: "Opus 4.8, Gemini 3.5, GPT-5.5, and a $965B valuation. The LLM race didn't slow down in June — it accelerated into something...

item.title: Prism: Make Your AI Editor Delegate
item.description: Prism: Make Your AI Editor Delegate By Bryan Barton Developer AI tooling is getting better, but a lot of workflows still have the same bad shape: Ask the premium model to do everything. Give it...

item.title: Serie 2: Resilient Architecture — Production Patterns
item.description: The Problem When a payment processing system fails to handle successful payments correctly, it can lead to inconsistent order states and frustrated customers. This implementation exists to ensure...

item.title: How to watch Nvidia’s Computex keynote
item.description: NVIDIA's CEO Jensen Huang is set to take the stage for his GTC Taipei keynote at 8PM PT / 11PM ET. You can watch all the announcements here and embedded below. Rumors have been flying about what...

item.title: Here’s your first look at ‘A Minecraft Movie Squared’ with Kirsten Dunst as Alex
item.description: The A Minecraft Movie sequel officially has a title: A Minecraft Movie Squared. What's more, we now know that Kirsten Dunst will star as Alex, the game's female character option, and that Matt...

item.title: Feeble Little Horse leans into digital weirdness on bitknot
item.description: From the opening moments of bitknot, it's obvious that Feeble Little Horse has found an entirely new gear. Where on Girl with Fish the blown-out textures were more '90s indie rock and shoegaze, on...

item.title: United flight forced to turn around because of a Bluetooth speaker name
item.description: United flight 236 from Newark to Palma de Mallorca on Saturday night was forced to turn around just an hour after takeoff due to security concerns around a Bluetooth signal. Multiple Redditors...

item.title: Making sense of the debate over AI psychosis
item.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether tech CEOs are "uniquely prone to AI psychosis."

item.title: This extravagant gaming laptop could ruin other screens for you
item.description: My eyes have seen the PC gaming promised land, and it's a beautifully bright world without a shred of blurriness. It's warm, it looks lovely, and it's impeccably sharp. Also, it's expensive as...

item.title: 007 First Light is already discounted for the PS5 and Steam
item.description: IO Interactive’s 007 First Light is here, and it’s just as stunning a James Bond mov — err, video game — as we hoped it would be. Pardon the confusion, the title’s engaging tutorial really feels...

item.title: Marathon’s second season is a chance for Bungie to turn things around
item.description: Earlier this month, I finally achieved the elusive goal I had set for myself in Bungie's Marathon. I collected six of the game's rarest items, allowing me to attempt and then successfully clear...

item.title: NASA confirms exploding meteor caused the sonic boom over Boston
item.description: On Saturday, at around 2:06 pm ET, a meteor streaked over the northeastern US and exploded north of Cape Cod Bay. The fireball was caught on camera by several people, shook houses, and can even be...

item.title: I went looking for the AI weed vape that gives you Bitcoin for smoking
item.description: The crypto weed vape found me on 4/20, the high holiday of cannabis enthusiasts everywhere. It arrived over Slack with the thumbnail of a man exhaling a plume of vapor, the words "every hit...

item.title: Dutch Authorities Dismantle Botnet Linked to 17 Million Infected Devices
item.description: Dutch authorities have announced the takedown of a botnet that enslaved millions of infected devices, including computers, tablets, smartphones, and IoT devices, to carry out malicious attacks....

item.title: User-replaceable batteries are coming back in a big way
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more news about gadgets and smartphones, follow Dominic Preston. The Stepback arrives in our...

item.title: How Turkey Hacked the Hair Transplant Industry
item.description: From specialized motors to the use of machine learning algorithms, Turkey’s billion-dollar hair-transplant industry is the result of a constant process of innovation.

item.title: SoftBank says it will invest up to €75 billion to build French data centers
item.description: The goal, the firm said, is to develop and operate up to 5 gigawatts of additional data center capacity.

item.title: ‘What a joke’: Github Copilot’s new token-based billing spurs consternation among devs
item.description: The golden age of Microsoft's Github Copilot appears to be at an end.

item.title: Meta is reportedly developing an AI pendant
item.description: Meta seems to be making big bets on AI-powered hardware.

item.title: I put Google’s 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark to work, and it’s actually pretty useful
item.description: Gemini Spark helps automate everyday tasks, from inbox summaries to local event planning, but it’s unclear why Google made it a separate product.

item.title: As the browser wars heat up, here are the hottest alternatives to Chrome and Safari in 2026
item.description: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

item.title: Do You Actually Need to Pay for Transcription Software?
item.description: I tested Wispr Flow and various AI-powered transcription software to see whether you should bother subscribing or stick with free services.
